response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software craftsman with ~14-16 years of hands-on engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, plus recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling. However, the role is a Director-level Principal Engineer focused on AI strategy, requiring recognized AI expertise, demonstrated AI products/platforms, deep cloud expertise, and a track record of advising strategic customers and shaping future cloud/AI direction — none of which are evident. Experience is at Senior/Staff IC level, not Principal/Director, and AI work appears recent and limited in scope.
strengths: Long, diverse engineering tenure across web, embedded, low-latency backends, and DevOps
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks
Demonstrated thought leadership through open-source projects, conference talks, and multi-module training courses
concerns: No recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products/platforms at the scale a Principal AI Strategy role demands
Cloud experience is limited (AWS CDK, Kubernetes mentions) — lacks depth across cloud architecture and strategic advisory
Career trajectory is Senior/Staff IC; no evidence of Director-level influence, customer/partner advisory, or org-wide strategic impact

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs. Director-level Principal IC scope
